verses.commentary: Potiphar bought him. Egyptian official. Captain of guard. From Ishmaelites.
The LORD was with Joseph. He prospered. Success in slavery. Key theme: LORD with Joseph.
Master saw LORD was with him. Even pagan Egyptian recognizes divine blessing.
Rapid rise. Trust built through faithfulness.

The blessing of the LORD was on everything Potiphar had, both in the house and in the field. From that time. LORD blessed Egyptian household because of Joseph. Blessing on everything. Like Laban blessed because of Jacob (30:27). God blesses those who bless his people.
Total trust. Like his mother Rachel (29:17).
Come to bed with me! Temptation. Direct proposition. Abuse of power.
He refused. Joseph's response. Loyalty to master.
Joseph frames it theologically: sin against God. Not just breaking social rules or betraying trust, but violating divine law. God-consciousness shapes his ethics.
He refused. Wise boundaries.
One day. Setup for crisis.
Come to bed! He left cloak. Ran out. Physical grab. Joseph flees. Second time Joseph loses a garment (37:23).
He left cloak. Ran out. Rejection complete. She has evidence.
I screamed. False accusation. Racism (this Hebrew). Reverses the story.
Left cloak. Ran out.
She kept his cloak beside her until his master came home.
She blames Potiphar. You brought him. Racial slur. Fabricated assault.
Left cloak. Ran out.
Joseph's faithfulness repaid with betrayal. Anger kindled.
Joseph in prison. Unjust punishment.
The LORD was with Joseph and showed him kindness and granted him favor in the eyes of the prison warden. LORD was with Joseph. Showed kindness (chesed, חֶסֶד). Same pattern. God's presence in prison.
Responsible for all. Another promotion.

The warden paid no attention to anything under Joseph's care, because the LORD was with Joseph and gave him success in whatever he did. LORD was with Joseph. Total trust again. Pattern repeated. Success follows Joseph because God is with him. God is preparing Joseph for greater leadership.
